platform: Next.js SPA with Nike Proprietary API

catalog_url.notes: Next.js SPA. __NEXT_DATA__ contains initialState.Wall with productGroupings.

list_page_url.notes: Browser render required. __NEXT_DATA__ contains full product data in Wall.productGroupings.

detail_url_pattern.notes: URL includes product slug, group key, and styleColor code. StyleColor encodes style + colorway.

pagination.mechanism: Nike product_wall API with anchor/count parameters
pagination.notes: Pagination via anchor parameter (offset). API requires consumerChannelId.

api_response_sample.notes: Each productGrouping contains multiple colorways as separate products.

title.example: Nike Sportswear - Women's Tight Graphic Tank Top

size.selector: Not available in listing page (detail page only)

reasons: Rich product data in __NEXT_DATA__ with title, price, color, images
Clear API pagination with 947 total products
Consistent URL pattern with styleColor codes
limitations: Size and description only on detail pages
SPA architecture means requests mode returns empty shell
clm_suitability.recommended_approach: browser mode + __NEXT_DATA__ extraction or direct API with consumerChannelId

blocking_issues: Direct API calls return 400/401 without correct parameters
Requests mode returns empty SPA shell
next_steps: Use browser mode to extract __NEXT_DATA__ from rendered pages
Reverse-engineer API authentication from browser network traffic
Test product_wall API with correct consumerChannelId
